intro:

However, I found a plant that closely resembles this name - "Ionthlaspis" seems to be a misspelling or variation of "Ionthlaspi" which seems to be an alternative name for "Thlaspi" a genus of plants in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Thlaspi, a type of wild mustard or field cress, is a small annual plant that grows in fields and along roadsides. The health benefits of Thlaspi are attributed to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.

The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi include glucosinolates, which are a group of sulfur-containing compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Other bioactive compounds found in Thlaspi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes.

Thlaspi is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures. It is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
